overrides.override_summary: California Corporations Code Section 31512 provides that any condition purporting to bind a franchisee to waive compliance with the Franchise Investment Law is void.
overrides.why_it_matters: Release signed at renewal/transfer cannot waive franchise law claims. Franchisee retains all statutory protections.

overrides.override_summary: The Franchise Agreement requires application of Minnesota law and forum. This provision may not be enforceable under California law.
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees may be able to litigate in California despite the Minnesota forum clause.

overrides.override_summary: Maximum interest rate charged in California is 10%.
overrides.why_it_matters: The FA's 1.5%/month (18% annual) interest rate is capped at 10% for California franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: No statement, questionnaire, or acknowledgment signed by franchisee shall waive claims under franchise law or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements. Franchisor may not disclaim representations or deny franchisee's ability to rely on them.
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects against post-sale disclaimers. The Franchisee Questionnaire cannot be used to limit franchisor liability.

overrides.override_summary: Provision terminating franchise on bankruptcy may not be enforceable under 11 USC §101.
overrides.why_it_matters: Bankruptcy alone may not be sufficient to terminate the franchise under federal bankruptcy law.

overrides.override_summary: California Franchise Relations Act (BPC 20000-20043) provides additional rights on transfer, termination, and non-renewal that may supersede FA provisions.
overrides.why_it_matters: California law provides stronger franchisee protections that override contractual terms.

overrides.override_summary: No waiver of franchise law claims (including fraud in the inducement) or disclaimer of reliance on franchisor statements.
overrides.why_it_matters: Same protection as California — franchisee questionnaires cannot limit franchisor liability.

overrides.override_summary: Any provision designating jurisdiction or venue outside Illinois is void for Illinois-enforceable actions. Arbitration outside Illinois is still permitted.
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois franchisees can litigate in Illinois courts despite Minnesota forum clause, but must still arbitrate in Minneapolis.

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law will govern to extent required by Rule 200.608 of Illinois Franchise Disclosure Laws.
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois franchise law supplements or overrides Minnesota law where applicable.

overrides.override_summary: Termination and non-renewal rights may be affected by 815 ILCS 705/19 and 705/20.
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois law provides additional protections on termination and non-renewal beyond what the FA provides.

overrides.override_summary: Any condition purporting to bind franchisee to waive compliance with Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act is void. No waiver of fraud-in-the-inducement claims.
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot contract away Illinois franchise law protections.

overrides.override_summary: 90 days' notice of termination with 60 days to cure (vs. 30-day/5-day standard). 180 days' notice of non-renewal.
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly longer notice and cure periods than the FA provides. More time to remedy defaults and plan for non-renewal.

overrides.override_summary: Minnesota Rule 2860.4400(D) prohibits franchisor from requiring franchisee to assent to a general release.
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota franchisees do not have to sign releases at renewal or transfer — a significant protection not available in other states.

overrides.override_summary: Prohibits jury trial waiver, liquidated damages, termination penalties, and judgment notes.
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee retains right to jury trial and cannot be subject to pre-determined damage amounts.

overrides.override_summary: Transfer will not be unreasonably withheld. Injunctive relief requires court determination of entitlement — cannot require consent to injunctive relief.
overrides.why_it_matters: More balanced transfer process. Court must decide injunctive relief rather than automatic consent.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor must protect franchisee's right to use trademarks or indemnify from any loss/costs arising from claims regarding Mark use.
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor has affirmative obligation to defend trademark rights, not just discretionary.

overrides.override_summary: All rights under GBL Article 33 remain in force at renewal and transfer. Non-waiver provisions of GBL §§687.4 and 687.5 must be satisfied.
overrides.why_it_matters: New York statutory franchise protections survive renewal and transfer, regardless of release language.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate the agreement on any grounds available by law (overriding 'Not applicable' in Item 17).
overrides.why_it_matters: New York franchisees have affirmative right to terminate — a major departure from the FA which provides no franchisee termination right.

overrides.override_summary: Choice of law/forum does not waive any right conferred by GBL Article 33.
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota choice of law cannot diminish New York franchise law protections.

overrides.override_summary: No waiver of claims under state franchise law, including fraud in the inducement. No disclaimer of reliance. Supersedes any other document term.
overrides.why_it_matters: Strongest available protection against post-sale disclaimers and waivers.

overrides.override_summary: Wisconsin Fair Dealership Law prohibits termination, cancellation, non-renewal, or substantial change in competitive circumstances without good cause. 90 days' prior written notice required; dealer has 60 days to cure.
overrides.why_it_matters: Very strong termination protections. Franchisor must demonstrate good cause and provide substantial notice period.
